# Claim/Evidence Matrix

This initial matrix contains one core placeholder claim per dynamic chapter.

Each claim has two separate classifications: a claim label that describes what kind of statement it is, and a support state that describes what currently supports it.

No claim is marked `source-derived`, `prototype-backed`, `synthetic-test-backed`, `empirical-test-backed`, or `external-literature-backed` yet. Those support states require source ingestion, prototype inspection, or actual test execution.

| Claim ID | Chapter ID | Claim | Claim label | Current support state | Assigned sources | Current evidence | Open gap |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.core` | `asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model` | Efficient ASI should be modeled as a governed stack of cooperating layers rather than as one undifferentiated model. | Design rationale | argument | `viea`, `beastbrain`, `aletheia`, `talos`, `moecot`, `scf` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `the-efficient-asi-hypothesis.core` | `the-efficient-asi-hypothesis` | Capability improves when cognition is routed, compressed, reused, governed, and tested with visible residuals. | Design rationale | argument | `viea`, `rmi`, `cgs`, `rankfold_neuralfold`, `bbvca_v9`, `simulation_scaling`, `beastbrain`, `beastbrain_timeless`, `aletheia`, `moecot` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `system-boundaries-and-authority.core` | `system-boundaries-and-authority` | Authority should be modeled as a typed, bounded capability attached to layers, fields, tools, artifacts, and principals. | Design rationale | argument | `viea`, `scf`, `talos`, `ladon_manhattan`, `genesiscode`, `moecot` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence.core` | `failure-modes-of-ungoverned-intelligence` | Ungoverned intelligence fails through stack-level breakdowns before dramatic catastrophic scenarios are needed. | Design rationale | argument | `scf`, `vcm_public`, `talos`, `spinoza`, `field_of_god`, `viea`, `simulation_scaling`, `vcm_editable`, `moecot` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `evidence-states-and-claim-discipline.core` | `evidence-states-and-claim-discipline` | Every major claim should carry both a claim label and a support state, and it should move only when source ingestion, prototype inspection, or actual tests justify the transition. | Design rationale | argument | `benchmaxxing`, `spinoza`, `viea`, `uat`, `coherence_exchange`, `verification_bandwidth` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `human-intent-as-a-formal-input.core` | `human-intent-as-a-formal-input` | Human intent should enter the stack as a structured contract with goals, constraints, authority, artifacts, evidence requirements, and stop conditions. | Design rationale | argument | `viea`, `software_magic_grimoire`, `planforge`, `cognitive_compilation`, `talos` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `constitutional-alignment-substrate.core` | `constitutional-alignment-substrate` | Alignment should function as a constitutional substrate whose commitments are operationalized as constraints on plans and system changes. | Design rationale | argument | `alignment_field`, `field_of_god`, `ethica_mechanica`, `eternal_code`, `coherence_exchange`, `spinoza` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `agency-dignity-and-corrigibility.core` | `agency-dignity-and-corrigibility` | A governed ASI stack should preserve human agency, dignity, corrigibility, and contestability as engineering requirements. | Design rationale | argument | `alignment_field`, `ethica_mechanica`, `field_of_god`, `eternal_code`, `coherence_exchange` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `moral-uncertainty-and-value-conflict.core` | `moral-uncertainty-and-value-conflict` | Value conflicts should be represented as explicit unresolved obligations, review paths, and bounded decisions rather than hidden inside reward functions. | Design rationale | argument | `ethica_mechanica`, `alignment_field`, `coherence_exchange`, `uat`, `spinoza` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `governance-rights-fork-exit-and-audit.core` | `governance-rights-fork-exit-and-audit` | Fork, exit, audit, dissent, and contestability should be treated as technical governance interfaces, not only political ideals. | Design rationale | argument | `ethica_mechanica`, `coherence_exchange`, `alignment_field`, `ladon_manhattan`, `spinoza`, `uat` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `stable-capability-fields.core` | `stable-capability-fields` | A stable capability field is a governed boundary with identity, interface, authority ceiling, qualification evidence, and rollback policy. | Design rationale | argument | `scf`, `viea`, `talos`, `ladon_manhattan`, `moecot` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `capability-replacement-and-rollback.core` | `capability-replacement-and-rollback` | Capability replacement should be an evidence-gated transaction with preconditions, regression checks, residual records, and rollback. | Design rationale | argument | `scf`, `rmi`, `benchmaxxing`, `cognitive_loop_closure`, `talos`, `moecot` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `security-kernel-and-digital-scifs.core` | `security-kernel-and-digital-scifs` | Sensitive context and privileged actions should be mediated by kernel-like security mechanisms and compartmentalized Digital SCIFs. | Design rationale | argument | `ladon_manhattan`, `context_engineer`, `talos`, `alignment_field`, `coherence_exchange` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `recursive-self-improvement-boundaries.core` | `recursive-self-improvement-boundaries` | Recursive self-improvement is allowed only as a bounded, evidence-gated, reversible transition over stable capability fields. | Design rationale | argument | `scf`, `benchmaxxing`, `rmi`, `alignment_field`, `viea`, `talos`, `moecot` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `intent-to-execution-contracts.core` | `intent-to-execution-contracts` | The stack should transform intent into execution contracts before any tool or runtime action occurs. | Design rationale | argument | `viea`, `talos`, `software_magic_grimoire`, `genesiscode`, `moecot` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `command-contracts-and-semantic-interfaces.core` | `command-contracts-and-semantic-interfaces` | Command contracts should use explicit semantic interfaces for objective, context, constraints, procedure, output, verification, and failure behavior. | Design rationale | argument | `software_magic_grimoire`, `viea`, `genesiscode`, `cognitive_compilation`, `talos` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `planning-as-a-control-layer.core` | `planning-as-a-control-layer` | Planning is a separate control layer that converts accepted goals into governed action without owning memory, reasoning, or side effects. | Design rationale | argument | `planforge`, `viea`, `cognitive_compilation`, `software_magic_grimoire`, `moecot` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `planforge-dags-and-intelligence-arbitrage.core` | `planforge-dags-and-intelligence-arbitrage` | PlanForge-style DAG planning can support intelligence arbitrage by assigning each node the minimum adequate capability and verification burden. | Design rationale | argument | `planforge`, `planforge_compiler_arch`, `coherence_exchange`, `cognitive_compilation`, `tokenmana`, `moecot` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.core` | `cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir` | Cognitive compilation should transform goals into semantic intermediate representations that can be optimized, verified, repaired, and targeted. | Design rationale | argument | `cognitive_compilation`, `planforge_compiler_arch`, `genesiscode`, `treellm`, `viea` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `virtual-context-abi.core` | `virtual-context-abi` | Virtual Context Memory should expose a Virtual Context ABI with stable addresses, versions, mounts, snapshots, fault classes, representation contracts, and lifecycle operations. | Design rationale | argument | `vcm_public`, `context_engineer`, `verification_bandwidth`, `viea`, `vcm_editable`, `moecot` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `semantic-pages-context-cells-and-certificates.core` | `semantic-pages-context-cells-and-certificates` | VCM should represent context as typed semantic pages or cells with evidence-carrying representation certificates. | Design rationale | argument | `vcm_public`, `verification_bandwidth`, `spinoza`, `context_engineer`, `vcm_editable` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-and-taint.core` | `context-transactions-snapshots-mounts-and-taint` | VCM should use transactional memory semantics: immutable events, versioned pages, snapshots, mounts, taint, temporal validity, and deletion closure. | Design rationale | argument | `vcm_public`, `ladon_manhattan`, `context_engineer`, `black_hole_context_manager`, `vcm_editable` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `verification-bandwidth-and-context-adequacy.core` | `verification-bandwidth-and-context-adequacy` | Context systems must distinguish generation capacity from verification bandwidth and declare when context is inadequate. | Design rationale | argument | `verification_bandwidth`, `vcm_public`, `spinoza`, `treellm`, `vcm_editable` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `claim-ledgers-and-belief-revision.core` | `claim-ledgers-and-belief-revision` | Reasoning should maintain claim ledgers with support states, provenance, contradiction links, uncertainty, and revision history. | Design rationale | argument | `spinoza`, `viea`, `coherence_exchange`, `aletheia`, `uat` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-claims.core` | `spinoza-verification-and-proof-carrying-claims` | Spinoza-style verification should convert high-value claims into proof-carrying or justification-carrying artifacts with explicit tiers. | Design rationale | argument | `spinoza`, `genesiscode`, `coherence_exchange`, `verification_bandwidth`, `treellm` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `unified-adaptive-tribunal-and-adversarial-review.core` | `unified-adaptive-tribunal-and-adversarial-review` | A tribunal layer should coordinate multiple reviewers, adversarial probes, consensus rules, and documented uncertainty. | Design rationale | argument | `uat`, `spinoza`, `coherence_exchange`, `talos`, `verification_bandwidth` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `labor-os-and-typed-jobs.core` | `labor-os-and-typed-jobs` | The execution layer should convert plans into typed jobs managed by a governed labor operating system. | Design rationale | argument | `talos`, `viea`, `genesiscode`, `software_magic_grimoire`, `talos_md`, `moecot` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `artifact-graphs-audit-logs-and-replay.core` | `artifact-graphs-audit-logs-and-replay` | Execution should produce an artifact graph with audit logs, provenance, replay metadata, and links to claims and tests. | Design rationale | argument | `talos`, `viea`, `cognitive_compilation`, `spinoza_composer`, `genesiscode`, `cognitive_loop_closure`, `moecot` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `runtime-adapters-tool-permissions-and-human-approval.core` | `runtime-adapters-tool-permissions-and-human-approval` | Runtime adapters should enforce typed permissions, sandboxing, human approval, and post-action evidence capture. | Design rationale | argument | `talos`, `viea`, `ladon_manhattan`, `software_magic_grimoire`, `genesiscode`, `moecot` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `procedural-memory-and-cognitive-loop-closure.core` | `procedural-memory-and-cognitive-loop-closure` | Cognitive loop closure compiles repeated reasoning into verified parameterized tools and procedural memory. | Design rationale | argument | `cognitive_loop_closure`, `rmi`, `rgs`, `benchmaxxing`, `talos`, `moecot` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `routing-heads-and-specialist-cores.core` | `routing-heads-and-specialist-cores` | ASI scales through a lightweight routing head that selects bounded specialist cores with local tools, memory, and authority. | Design rationale | argument | `octopus_router`, `rmi`, `beastbrain`, `cognitive_loop_closure`, `rgs`, `moecot` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `readiness-gates-residual-escrow-and-quarantine.core` | `readiness-gates-residual-escrow-and-quarantine` | Readiness gates and residual escrow should govern when modules are promoted, quarantined, split, merged, retired, or retrained. | Design rationale | argument | `rmi`, `benchmaxxing`, `scf`, `octopus_router`, `cognitive_loop_closure`, `moecot` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `moecot-runtime-and-multi-core-orchestration.core` | `moecot-runtime-and-multi-core-orchestration` | MoECOT should be treated as the implementation-reference layer for a governed low-parameter multi-core ASI stack once its authenticated source is fully mined. | Design rationale | argument | `octopus_router`, `rmi`, `benchmaxxing`, `beastbrain`, `viea`, `scf`, `talos`, `moecot_md`, `moecot` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `compact-generative-systems-and-residual-honesty.core` | `compact-generative-systems-and-residual-honesty` | A compact generative system is useful only when compactness, generativity, governance, verification, and residual burden are all explicit. | Design rationale | argument | `cgs`, `rgs`, `bugbrain`, `simulation_scaling`, `rmi` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `generate-verify-repair-compression.core` | `generate-verify-repair-compression` | Generate-verify-repair compression should store the smallest useful lawful explanation plus the cheapest exact remainder when generation fails. | Design rationale | argument | `bbvca_v9`, `bbvca_main`, `cgs`, `rankfold_neuralfold` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `rankfold-neuralfold-and-artifact-compression.core` | `rankfold-neuralfold-and-artifact-compression` | RankFold/NeuralFold-style artifact compression belongs as a bounded implementation hypothesis with probe-route fallback and residual coding. | Design rationale | argument | `rankfold_neuralfold`, `rankfold_compressor`, `bbvca_v9`, `cgs`, `bugbrain` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `semantic-representation-and-tree-structured-models.core` | `semantic-representation-and-tree-structured-models` | Tree-structured semantic representations should be treated as optional representation substrates that must prove grounding, utility, and interoperability. | Design rationale | argument | `treellm`, `spinoza`, `verification_bandwidth`, `cognitive_compilation`, `cgs` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `resource-economics-and-token-budgets.core` | `resource-economics-and-token-budgets` | The stack should account for token budgets, verification tax, load stability, and risk-adjusted inference value. | Design rationale | argument | `tokenmana`, `planforge`, `coherence_exchange`, `simulation_scaling`, `viea` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `simulation-fidelity-and-physical-constraints.core` | `simulation-fidelity-and-physical-constraints` | Simulation and fidelity claims should be bounded by resource constraints and used as design constraints rather than assumed unlimited substrates. | Design rationale | argument | `simulation_scaling`, `cgs`, `rankfold_neuralfold`, `tokenmana`, `alignment_field` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `mathematical-and-search-substrates.core` | `mathematical-and-search-substrates` | Coils, calculi, and geometric search belong in the stack as optional specialist substrates until tests show where they improve search, routing, or compression. | Design rationale | argument | `genesiscode`, `temporal_coil_research`, `cognitive_compilation`, `treellm`, `simulation_scaling`, `coilmoecot` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `executable-specifications-and-lean-proof-envelope.core` | `executable-specifications-and-lean-proof-envelope` | Lean proofs and executable specs should target small invariants: authority, support states, SCF replacement, job lifecycle, context adequacy, and residual records. | Design rationale | argument | `genesiscode`, `spinoza`, `scf`, `talos`, `viea` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `benchmark-ratchets-and-anti-goodhart-evidence.core` | `benchmark-ratchets-and-anti-goodhart-evidence` | Benchmark ratchets should preserve regressions, create harder frontiers, record residuals, and resist Goodhart pressure. | Design rationale | argument | `benchmaxxing`, `rmi`, `cognitive_loop_closure`, `uat`, `coherence_exchange`, `tokenmana`, `moecot`, `road_to_agi` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `integrated-reference-architecture.core` | `integrated-reference-architecture` | The stack can be specified as an integrated reference architecture with typed handoffs, authority stops, artifacts, evidence updates, and self-improvement gates. | Design rationale | argument | `viea`, `scf`, `vcm_public`, `planforge`, `talos`, `spinoza`, `octopus_router`, `rmi`, `benchmaxxing`, `alignment_field`, `cgs`, `cognitive_loop_closure`, `moecot`, `vcm_editable` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `prototype-roadmap.core` | `prototype-roadmap` | The prototype should grow in staged increments from source matrix and artifact graph to governed capability replacement. | Design rationale | argument | `viea`, `benchmaxxing`, `scf`, `vcm_public`, `planforge`, `beastbrain`, `beastbrain_timeless`, `bugbrain`, `moecot`, `moecot_md`, `road_to_agi`, `coherence_exchange` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `living-book-methodology.core` | `living-book-methodology` | The living book should model the ASI stack discipline through manifest-driven Quarto, source queues, claim/evidence matrices, proof manifests, tests, changelogs, and releases. | Design rationale | argument | `benchmaxxing`, `viea`, `bugbrain`, `cognitive_loop_closure`, `moecot`, `moecot_md`, `road_to_agi` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `open-research-agenda-and-bibliography-plan.core` | `open-research-agenda-and-bibliography-plan` | The bibliography and research agenda should map source families, external literature queues, missing papers, proof targets, experiment backlogs, and chapter insertion rules. | Design rationale | argument | `verification_bandwidth`, `benchmaxxing`, `alignment_field`, `scf`, `planforge`, `vcm_public`, `spinoza`, `talos`, `rmi`, `cgs`, `genesiscode`, `simulation_scaling`, `moecot`, `coilmoecot`, `road_to_agi`, `vcm_editable` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |

## Claim Labels

| Label | Meaning |
|---|---|
| Demonstrated | Shown by a recorded artifact, derivation, implementation, or source-backed example in scope. |
| Measured | Quantified by a recorded measurement or benchmark result. |
| Mechanized | Expressed as runnable code, an executable schema, or a formal proof target. |
| Hypothesized | Proposed as a testable claim that still needs evidence. |
| Design rationale | An architectural choice supported by reasoning and constraints. |
| Speculative | Exploratory or conjectural; do not use as a deployed system guarantee. |

## Support States

| State | Meaning |
|---|---|
| unsupported | Not yet supported; likely remove or mark as speculative. |
| argument | Supported by reasoning only. |
| source-derived | Derived from supplied source papers. |
| prototype-backed | Implemented in a prototype but not robustly tested. |
| synthetic-test-backed | Supported by controlled synthetic tests. |
| empirical-test-backed | Supported by external or realistic tests. |
| external-literature-backed | Supported by third-party literature. |
| deprecated | Superseded, merged, or retired; retained for lineage and review. |
| refuted | Contradicted by later evidence or tests; retained to preserve negative results. |
